title: Did it matter for Gendo whether it was nerv or seeles plan
display_title: Did it matter for Gendo whether it was nerv or seeles plan
wikitext:
[]: Did it matter for Gendo whether it was nerv or seele's plan?             Quote Post by xeon » Mon Jul 20, 2009 10:35 am Gendo basically wanted to reunite with his dead wife and wanted to do that through 3I. Did it make a difference if seele's HIP was used? Wouldn't he still be able to reunite with her with both cases? Its still vague to me.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Jul 20, 2009 10:38 am No, if Seele's plan had worked Gendo would have been united with everyone not with Yui. This is exactly what he didn't want.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jul 20, 2009 5:27 pm Being able to reunite with your wife and see her warm smile again isn't the same as merging everyone on the planet into one mindless, pointless superbeing.

[]:             Quote Post by xeon » Mon Jul 20, 2009 8:27 pm wouldn't third impact just destroy everything...meaning all gendo would get is death. If he wanted to meet his wife in death then why not just hang himself.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jul 20, 2009 8:56 pm 1) Hanging himself won't work, Yui's soul is in the Evangelion. 2) Third Impact isn't a "kill everything" button, it's basically a chance to reshape the world to your liking. It only leads to killing everything if that's what the person initiating it wants. Gendo just wanted to bring Yui back to life or something.

[]:             Quote Post by xeon » Mon Jul 20, 2009 9:31 pm seele uses anti AT field to dissolve everyone into one to produce something perfect...basically a state of nothingness. 3I would allow its initiator to create its own world (god like power)...so if an Angel was to make contact they would choose to destroy all humans. And gendo would revive yui...but Rei became the initiator and gave Shinji the decision.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 20, 2009 10:07 pm xeon wrote: And gendo would revive yui...

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Tue Jul 21, 2009 9:51 am i thought that was an obvious one. he wanted HIS 3I not Seele's. he did it all for the yui.

[]:             Quote Post by TheWaffleCult » Fri Jul 24, 2009 4:32 am But if he reunited with his wife, what else would happen? Is that it? This is something I'm still trying to wrap my head around constantly; I've been watching Evangelion with a friend who hasn't seen it before, and I honestly come up blank whenever a question about this particular series of motives is questioned. Could all of the tanging and such have been avoided if Rei III had submitted to Gendo? Or am I missing something?

[]:             Quote Post by Armitage » Fri Jul 24, 2009 4:54 am I honestly understood it as not only would he be reunited with his wife , but perhaps it might have been similar to Shinji and Asuka : that Gendo would choose to 'get everyone out of the way' and live on the planet with his wife . If we refer to the Eva as an Ark as Reichu says then it can be used in two senses (since there were two senses of the Ark in the Bible) either it would be the ark that would save the chosen from the flood (and in this case since Man and Eva would be standing in place of God , they would be the ones who choose who gets "tanged" as people call it) or it would be the Ark that contains something Holy and in essence also becomes untouchable (which is an interesting way of looking at Unit 01 at the end of EoE as it floats out into space) . In SEELE's plan and in one alternative for Shinji , humanity would have lost individuality (an interesting concept as it mirrors the 'singularity' point intimations of much of the series) and thus not only would Gendo 'lose himself' but also lose Yui despite being integrated with each other . Drawing this out it is actually staggering : consider how much you or I have been integrated through the bio-technology of the internet (biological entities and electronic tools working in tandum) creating a sense of , what I dub , "Community without Communion" . Human interaction on an individual basis is what ENGAGES the human person fully . Internet interaction not only disallows that but makes it prone to: dissociative Anonymity (We are Anonymous , we never forgive , we never forget etc etc) , Solipsistic Introjection (which we see in the series a lot actually as a microcosm of this phenomenon growing right now) , Dissociative Imagination (which derives from the modern technology's amazing ability to reinforce our mise-en-scene post-modern interpretation of reality) . This is also why the title Evangelion was important because the gospel message was spread through witnesses and evangelists which is an interpersonal and deeply moving experience for the Early Christians as well as continuing on today (not including the stupid televangelism etc) . Christian theology explains this because of the Incarnation of Christ and thus why it is ONLY through human interaction that one's humanity is fully engaged . This is why many consider it insufficient to have newsletters , pamphlets etc explaining religion . Another reason how Anno's choice of title ties into this neurotic frustration over individualism and communion : something which , unlike in the Western Christian tradition finds itself an answer in what is called "The Sacramental Life", is unfortunately absent in Japan because of its "the world is as good as it gets" Shinto tradition coupled with the post-World War II "spiritual diaspora." Anno's work in 1995 initiates this even before the internet becomes hyper-realized mostly because Japan's urbanisation rivals if not surpasses most any other country . Its society , forced (because that was the only way it could survive Post-WW2) into tight knit quarters but neuroticised by its strict social constructs as well as a denial of communion with one another makes it extremely prone to despair and existential crisis over its "community without communion" or if I were to put it more in layman's terms: "the apartment complex" in which case many individual rooms are corporated into a whole while maintaining barriers (eva geeks would call this "Our AT Fields") Thus , Gendo's quest is not so unlike his sons , but whereas Gendo has become too old and cynical about it , Shinji retains a sense of his innocence that allows him to force his way to a more mature framework of the world (albeit that's up for debate itself) . Both would reject SEELE's plan because both Gendo and Shinji want communion , not just community .

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 24, 2009 6:42 am Armitage, your interpretation corroborates on every point with the evaotaku EoE FAQ. So I have to ask, is that where you got it from, or did you come to those conclusions independently?

[]:             Quote Post by Armitage » Fri Jul 24, 2009 6:46 am Monk Ed wrote: Armitage, your interpretation corroborates on every point with the evaotaku EoE FAQ. So I have to ask, is that where you got it from, or did you come to those conclusions independently?

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Fri Jul 24, 2009 8:21 am Reichu wrote: xeon wrote: And gendo would revive yui...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 24, 2009 9:24 am Reichu wrote: I guess he and Yui would get a room to themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Fri Jul 24, 2009 12:18 pm TheWaffleCult wrote: Could all of the tanging and such have been avoided if Rei III had submitted to Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Fri Jul 24, 2009 12:59 pm I doubt that Gendo really cares one way or another what happens outside his reuniting with Yui. Though certainly if he had Yui, and both Fruits and SoLs and a nice sixpack, he'd start getting ideas... EDIT: Armitage wrote: communion , not just community .

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Jul 24, 2009 1:29 pm TheWaffleCult wrote: Could all of the tanging and such have been avoided if Rei III had submitted to Gendo? Or am I missing something?

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Fri Jul 24, 2009 2:04 pm That makes a lot of sense to me, Merridian. It sounds like something Fuyutsuki could agree with.

[]:             Quote Post by TheWaffleCult » Fri Jul 24, 2009 4:27 pm Lots of interesting theories here. Not sure if I'm any less confused, but I like these ideas! The "Death creates nothing" line is extremely important, I can't believe I missed that.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 24, 2009 4:31 pm What Merridian said, totally. Fits with the Eva=Ark thing completely. I imagine that souls that didn't re-embody would stick inside Unit 01, Gendo being one of them.
